acceptance_targets: [CH07_CONTINUITY_WEEKEND_TIMELINE] Pass only if a cold reader can reconstruct one coherent sequence from the revised chapter alone: the group arrives and drafts on Thursday, Martin dies early Saturday, Doug retrieves the bottles around 3 A.M. Saturday, and no line in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:7, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:75, or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:127 implies a Friday-arrival or Friday-draft version of events.

[CH07_AWARD_DOCK_OVEREXPLAINS] Pass only if the revised dock passage lands Doug's gratitude-for-showing-up and Martin-memory once, then exits within one further beat on a concrete action or image. If material equivalent to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:187 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:193 remains, it must introduce new pressure beyond restating that Doug is depressed, hidden, and barely hanging on; otherwise the scene still fails.
[PROSE_FORMULA_CH07] Pass if chapter 07 contains zero instances of 'the specific [noun]' and Doug's interiority uses concrete image or selective silence instead. Fail if any instances remain.
[RED-001] Chapter 7's opening does not re-describe Doug's gauntness, bags under eyes, or ill-fitting hoodie in terms synonymous with Chapter 1.
[RED-010] Chapter 7 does not restate Mallick's height or build.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if a cold reader can reconstruct one coherent sequence from the revised chapter alone: the group arrives and drafts on Thursday, Martin dies early Saturday, Doug retrieves the bottles around 3 A.M. Saturday, and no line in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:7, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:75, or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:127 implies a Friday-arrival or Friday-draft version of events.
findings.problem: The chapter places the group's arrival and the draft-night memories on Friday, but the continuity sheet canon has the men arriving and drafting on Thursday, with Martin dying early Saturday. Line 7 also says the bottles sat upstairs for 'approximately fourteen hours' before Martin died, which conflicts with both the canonical weekend timeline and this chapter's own 3 A.M. retrieval. This is a direct continuity contradiction, not a permissible ambiguity.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:7, reset the bottle-stashing beat to the canonical Thursday arrival and adjust the elapsed-time language so it matches a Saturday-early-morning death. At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:75 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:127, replace the Friday-arrival/Friday-draft phrasing with canon-accurate references to draft night and the later fatal night so the weekend sequence is internally and globally consistent.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if the revised dock passage lands Doug's gratitude-for-showing-up and Martin-memory once, then exits within one further beat on a concrete action or image. If material equivalent to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:187 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:193 remains, it must introduce new pressure beyond restating that Doug is depressed, hidden, and barely hanging on; otherwise the scene still fails.
findings.problem: The dock sequence reaches its emotional arrival by the time Doug realizes he is glad he came and that Martin had understood the shape of his unhappiness, then keeps re-proving the same point through additional thesis-heavy explanation. The result is a good character chapter that still reads more controlled in outline than on the page: instead of trusting the lake, Martin-memory, and return to the basement to land the turn, the prose keeps annotating Doug's depression after the turn has already happened.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress the dock close centered on snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:177, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:181, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:183, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:187, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:193. Preserve the must-land beats in that material: Doug is unexpectedly glad he came, Martin's July call mattered, and he returns inside unable to sleep. After those beats land, either end the chapter on the concrete dock-to-basement movement already present or replace the remaining explanation with one genuinely new pressure, decision, or consequence. Do not spend a second pass translating the same sadness into abstract summary at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:187 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:193.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter 07 contains zero instances of 'the specific [noun]' and Doug's interiority uses concrete image or selective silence instead. Fail if any instances remain.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1753 'the specific way she held her mug with both hands'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1799 omitted long line containing 'the specific'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1897 omitted long line containing 'the specific'

evidence.excerpt: 1753: Doug took a mug from the cabinet — the blue one with the chip on the handle, the mug nobody else wanted, which was why it was always available — and poured. Black. He'd stopped taking cream when the weight started falling off, not as a diet choice but because the taste of coffee with cream reminded him of weekend mornings with Steph, her feet on the couch, the specific way she held her mug with both hands and blew across the surface even when it wasn't that hot.

1799: Doug took his coffee to the great room and sat in the corner of the sectional, close to the window where the morning light came in low and gold off the lake. The draft board was still on the wall. Martin's picks were circled in red marker — the shorthand they'd used for twenty-five years to mark the commissioner's selections. Martin had taken Kelce in the second round, which everyone had groaned about, and Lamar Jackson in the third, which was a reach that had sparked a five-minute argument, and in the seventh round he'd taken a rookie receiver nobody had heard of, and Nate had done a whole bit about it — who is this guy, where did you find this guy, are you drafting him or adopting him. Martin had just smiled. Martin's smile was the thing Doug missed most, which surprised him because he would have guessed he'd miss the conversations — Martin was the only one who asked questions that weren't setups for his own answers. But it was the smile. The gap between Martin's front teeth when he was pleased with himself, which was most of the time, because Martin moved through the world with the specific confidence of a man who'd inherited a lake house and built a life that fit inside i...

1897: Doug thought about his apartment. The one-bedroom in Richfield with the beige carpet and the neighbor who watched *Jeopardy!* at a volume that suggested either hearing loss or hostility. He'd moved in the week after Steph said she was done, and the apartment had the specific emptiness of a place where no one had ever been happy — not actively miserable, just absent of any history that might soften the walls. He'd hung nothing on them. He'd bought a mattress and a coffeemaker and a set of towels. The towels were gray because gray was the color you bought when you didn't care about the color of your towels and wanted to stop standing in the aisle at Target.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 1753: Doug took a mug from the cabinet — the blue one with the chip on the handle, the mug nobody else wanted, which was why it was always available — and poured. Black. He'd stopped taking cream when the weight started falling off, not as a diet choice but because the taste of coffee with cream reminded him of weekend mornings with Steph, her feet on the couch, the specific way she held her mug with both hands and blew across the surface even when it wasn't that hot.

findings.problem: Three instances of 'the specific' in Doug's chapter, where the construction is particularly damaging because Doug's voice should be the quietest and most observational — mechanical intensifiers undercut his distinctive stillness.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1753, 1799, 1897. Replace all three. Doug's interiority should render sensation through image and omission, not through the narrator's intensifier. At line 1753, 'the specific way she held her mug' can become concrete — show Steph's grip, the steam, the temperature of the memory — without the formula.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter 7's opening does not re-describe Doug's gauntness, bags under eyes, or ill-fitting hoodie in terms synonymous with Chapter 1.
findings.evidence: Ch 1 line 27: 'His face was thinner, the hoodie hung wrong on him, and the bags under his eyes looked permanent'; Ch 7 line 1731: 'Six feet of gaunt. Bags under his eyes that hadn't been there in June. The hoodie hanging off his shoulders like it was still on the hanger'
findings.problem: Doug's gauntness/bags under eyes/hoodie hanging wrong are established in Ch 1 (line 27). Ch 7 restates all three traits in near-synonymous phrasing. This is the first of 8 separate descriptions of Doug's weight loss across the novel.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut or compress the physical re-description at line 1731. Keep 'twenty-seven pounds since the divorce' as new information; cut the re-inventory of gaunt/bags/hoodie that the reader already has from Ch 1.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter 7 does not restate Mallick's height or build.
findings.evidence: Ch 4 line 912: 'five-six, stocky, gray-streaked brown hair pulled back in a low bun, reading glasses on a beaded chain'; Ch 7 line 1819: 'five-six, stocky, the gray-streaked bun slightly loose on one side'
findings.problem: Ch 7 re-describes Mallick's height (five-six), build (stocky), and hair (gray-streaked bun) from her Ch 4 introduction.
findings.rewrite_direction: At line 1819, cut height and build restatement. Doug can note something new about Mallick — the pen she clicks, her demeanor — without restating basic physical stats.

findings.evidence: 44 em-dash-containing prose lines / 102 total prose lines = 43.1%.

findings.problem: Ch 7 He-opener density is 15.1%. Doug's POV chapter relies heavily on 'He' openers.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary sentence openings. Reduce He-openers to below 7%.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: The comedy is the creative risk and the creative achievement. These are men in their late forties who talk the way men in their late forties talk when there are no women present and the drinks have been flowing for hours: crude, raunchy, profane, sexually explicit, casually cruel, and occasionally accidentally beautiful. Do not clean this up. Do not soften the language. Do not make them witty where they would be vulgar. Do not make them eloquent where they would be clumsy. The profanity is not decoration — it is the texture of the friendship. When the second death breaks the comic register, the absence of that profane warmth should feel like a loss, not a relief. The murder, when it arrives, should be rendered with physical specificity — the water, the struggle, the sounds. The grief should be specific. The shame should be specific. The twenty-two-year-old guilt should be embodied, not summarized. If a scene makes the writer uncomfortable, that is a signal to stay in it longer, not to cut away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Every euphemism for death, sex, bodily function, emotional state, or financial desperation must be replaced with the direct term or a character-specific avoidance that itself reveals something about the character. Cliff saying 'restructure' when he means 'I'm broke' is character work. The narrator describing Cliff as 'facing financial challenges' is sanitization.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the absolute norm. These are informal men in their late forties who have been friends for twenty-five years. Uncontracted forms should appear only for deliberate emphasis, Joel's occasional legal precision, or Mallick's public-persona overcorrection. If two consecutive lines of dialogue contain uncontracted forms and neither character has a stated reason for formality, at least one is wrong.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. After a dialogue-heavy chapter, lead the next with interiority or description. After an interview chapter, give the reader a memory or a quiet observation. After a comic set piece, let the next chapter breathe. The scene counts vary (2-3) and should feel varied — a two-scene chapter after a three-scene chapter creates natural pacing shift. The tonal break at Chapter 14 should feel like a gear change, not a gradual dimming.
prose_style_profile.diction: Muscular, specific, unpretentious. No literary posturing. The prose should sound like intelligent conversation, not workshopped fiction. Prefer concrete nouns over abstract ones. Prefer active constructions. Avoid decorative metaphor — when a metaphor appears, it should be load-bearing. The comedy demands precision: funny is specific, and vague is never funny.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Prestige-literary inflation — do not elevate the comic register into literary solemnity. A man flushing Viagra while a detective knocks on the door is funny. The prose should be funny. Do not describe it as if it were a scene from Dostoevsky.

Comedic exhaustion — do not let every paragraph contain a joke. Comedy needs rhythm, which means it needs non-comic beats to land against. Let some paragraphs be straightforwardly descriptive or emotional.
Interior monologue sprawl — close third is not stream of consciousness. The POV character's interiority should be selective and purposeful, not an open channel of every thought.
Simultaneous narration — do not describe characters 'simultaneously' feeling multiple things. Choose the dominant feeling and let the undertone be implicit.
Forensic procedure porn — this is not a police procedural. Keep investigation details specific but brief. The comedy and character are the vehicle, not the forensics.

Metaphor creep — do not let the same metaphor template appear more than twice in a chapter. If you've used a sports metaphor, a water metaphor, and a fire metaphor, check that none of them are doing the same structural work.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV. Each chapter is locked to one character's interiority. No omniscient intrusions. The prose should take on the coloring of the POV character without becoming parody — Nate's chapters move faster and list more, Doug's chapters are quieter and more observational, Mallick's chapters shift between her two registers.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Conversational propulsion, not literary drift. The prose should move like someone telling you this story — not rushed, but never self-admiring. Vary sentence length aggressively: long compound sentences for the comic set pieces, short declarative hits for the turns. The humor lives in rhythm as much as content — the timing of a punchline is a prose-level decision.
sensory_bias: auditory — conversations, lake sounds, house creaks, the specific silence of a house where someone died
proprioceptive — hangovers, physical discomfort, adrenaline, the specific sensation of a body that hasn't slept
olfactory — booze, lake water, morning coffee, the basement must, the casserole
